Explanation:
The plan is to rotate Spot 90 degrees to the left, move 1 meter, rotate 90 degrees to the right, move 1 meter, and then call LLM to replan. This sequence allows Spot to safely navigate around the obstacle. 

The first rotation is to move Spot away from the obstacle, and the second rotation is to face the target. The two moves are short and bounded, and the plan will be re-evaluated after each move to check if the target is reached. If the target is reached, the task is finished. If an obstacle is detected ahead, the plan will be modified to rotate around it.

The revised plan is to rotate Spot 180 degrees to face the target, move 1 meter, rotate 90 degrees to the right, move 1 meter, and then call LLM to replan. This sequence is more concrete and should help Spot navigate around the obstacle. 

Note that the first rotation is to face the target, and the two moves are short and bounded. The plan will be re-evaluated after each move to check if the target is reached. If the target is reached, the task is finished. If an obstacle is detected ahead, the plan will be modified to rotate around it.
